data.title: Sagtec Global expects 64% YoY revenue growth
data.description:
• Sagtec Global (SAGT) announced on Wednesday that the company currently estimates 64% year-on-year revenue growth, a 75% increase in net profit, and a 93% jump in operating cash flow, based on preliminary internal management estimates.
  • The company said it intends to focus on strengthening its core technology offerings in 2026, while selectively pursuing growth opportunities that align with its long-term strategy and risk management framework.
  • The firm said that it will prioritize evaluation and development of AI platform opportunities, including a planned collaboration with Skilliks. 
  • Sagtec Global also reiterated its previously announced strategic partnership with HM Development. Pursuant to this partnership, the firm invested $2M into HM’s AI Edutech Power Trading software through the issuance of Class A ordinary shares. 
  • SAGT +3.64% premarket to $1.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Agnico Eagle to sell Gunnarn Mining to Goldsky in cash and stock deal
data.description:
Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) said Wednesday it agreed to sell the 55% of Gunnarn Mining it did not already own to Goldsky Resources (GSKRF) for US$20M in cash, the issuance of more than 75.5M common shares of Goldsky at C$2.64/share, and the grant of a 2% net smelter return royalty on the Barsele project in Sweden.

Agnico Eagle (AEM) said divesting its direct interest in the Barsele project aligns with its prioritization of its high-quality internal project pipeline; as additional exploration work and studies will be needed to advance Barsele toward development, the company believes the project will benefit from being Goldsky's (GSKRF) primary focus.

Following execution of the agreement, Agnico Eagle (AEM) will beneficially own more than 82.8M common shares of Goldsky (GSKRF), or ~32.5% of the issued and outstanding shares on a non-diluted basis.

data.title: Fed rate decision preview: Markets expect a hold, but who dissents?
data.description:
The Federal Reserve is set to announce its latest interest rate decision this afternoon, and while markets overwhelmingly expect policymakers to hold rates steady in the 3.50%–3.75% range, attention is increasingly shifting to a different question:

Who, if anyone, will dissent from the majority view? 

According to pricing on Kalshi, three FOMC members stand out as the most likely to break ranks. Stephen Miran is viewed as the clear outlier, with markets assigning a 94% probability that he will dissent. Michelle Bowman follows with a 62% chance, while Christopher Waller is close behind at 57%. 

Beyond those three, the likelihood drops off sharply, with the next highest probability of dissent sitting at 14% with Anna Paulson. 

While the policy outcome itself may be predictable, the voting breakdown could offer valuable insight into internal debates shaping the Fed’s path forward. 

See what prediction markets are forecasting on who will dissent: 

  1. Stephen Miran — 94%
  2. Michelle Bowman — 62%
  3. Christopher Waller — 57%
  4. Anna Paulson — 14%
  5. Neel Kashkari — 13% 
  6. Lisa Cook — 6% (tie) 
  7. Beth Hammack — 6% (tie)
  8. Philip Jefferson — 4%
  9. John Williams — 3% 
  10. Lorie Logan — 2%
  11. Jerome Powell — <1% 
  12. Michael Barr — (no probability listed / effectively unpriced)

Index Focused ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ). 

Fixed Income Focused ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: Brown & Brown downgraded to Neutral at Citi on slow rebound
data.description:
Citi downgraded Brown & Brown (BRO) to Neutral from Buy as an expected 2026 growth rebound "appears more modest than we anticipated," analyst Matthew Heimermann wrote in a note to clients on Wednesday.

The bank's Buy thesis depended on 2025 growth weakness being transient and reflecting more of an industry issue than specific to Brown & Brown (BRO).

Brown & Brown may be turning a corner, but the pace of improvement is slow. "While retail growth of 2.0-3.0% is within the error rate of implied industry growth rates, specialty distribution looks like it will under-grow the E&S (excess & surplus) market (by ~100 bps), which implies continued mix (or program maturity/capacity) headwinds have not totally abated," he explained.

"We are also unable to identify a convincing catalyst to improve 12-month risk-reward as talent competition risks persist," he added.

"Do we worry we might be bottom-ticking the stock?" Heimermann asked. "Yes. However, our previous thesis appears incorrect, and we find it more difficult to differentiate the stock versus the group."

Citi's Neutral rating on BRO aligns with the SA Quant rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Hold and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

data.title: California BanCorp GAAP EPS of $0.50, revenue of $45.9M
data.description:
• California BanCorp press release (BCAL): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.50.
  • Revenue of $45.9M.

  • Total assets at December 31, 2025 were $4.03 billion, a decrease of $67.8 million or 1.7% from September 30, 2025. The decrease in total assets from the prior quarter was primarily related to a decrease in cash and cash equivalents of $159.3 million, partially offset by increases in available-for-sale debt securities of $25.5 million and loans, including loans held for sale, of $62.0 million, as compared to the prior quarter.

data.title: Celestica rises as BofA starts with Buy rating ahead of earnings
data.description:
Celestica (CLS) shares rose 3.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday as Bank of America initiated coverage on the Canadian technology firm with a Buy rating ahead of its fourth-quarter results. 

“We think this leading provider of white-box switches will be a beneficiary of an AI-driven upgrade boom for high-speed data center switches, and strong growth in custom ASIC accelerated servers,” Bank of America analysts wrote in a note to clients. “CLS has consistently maintained first-mover advantage in 400G, 800G and 1.6T switching, and we expect continued market share gains. F27 should be a strong year with many new programs ramping. We are above Street on F27 earnings and see upside to consensus estimates.”

In addition to the Buy rating, Bank of America put a $400 price target on Celestica. The analysts said they believe the stock is “still undervalued,” despite the sharp run in the last year, as earnings could grow at a 47% annual rate through 2027. 

The company may also benefit from OpenAI's (OPENAI) deal with Broadcom (AVGO) to deploy 10 gigawatts of OpenAI-designed AI accelerators, the analysts said. “This project allows Celestica to showcase its full-suite of ODM capabilities in design, manufacturing, orchestration and deployment. We expect it will drive multi-billion dollars of revenue for CLS in 2027 and beyond.”

Celestica is set to report fourth-quarter results tomorrow before trading opens. A consensus of analysts expects the company to earn $1.76 per share on $3.49B in revenue.

data.title: IonQ completes acquisition of Skyloom
data.description:
• IonQ (IONQ) on Wednesday announced the completion of its acquisition of Skyloom Global, a U.S.-based developer of lightwave-optics technology.
  • The transaction, previously announced in November 2025, has now closed, the company said.
  • "The acquisition strengthens IonQ’s quantum networking roadmap by adding Skyloom’s deep expertise in free-space optical communications, photonic systems engineering, and secure data transmission. These capabilities are expected to accelerate IonQ’s work across quantum networking, quantum key distribution, and future quantum-enabled communications infrastructure," the company said.
  • IONQ +1.25% premarket to $46.06.
  • Source: Press Release
